title: Chapter 3: The Wolf King Is Paralyzed — My Turn
content: The poisoning happened on a Thursday.

Sera would remember that detail later — the absurd ordinariness of a Thursday — because nothing else about that day was ordinary. It started with tea, as most of her mornings did. Earl Grey, steeped for exactly four minutes, served in the Alpha's private study at precisely seven in the morning.

She knew the routine by now. Twelve days of serving the Wolf King had taught her his patterns. He rose before dawn. He read reports until breakfast. He terrified his advisors until lunch. He trained with Darius until dinner. And through all of it, his Inner Wolf provided a running commentary that made Sera's days an exercise in not bursting into laughter at the worst possible moments.
This morning, however, something was wrong.

She felt it the moment she stepped into the study. A wrongness in the air — something sharp and chemical beneath the familiar scent of old books and leather. Kael was already at his desk, but his posture was off. Too stiff. Too still.
"Sera." His voice was normal. Cold. Controlled. "The tea."
She crossed the room, set the tray on his desk, and poured.
*"I don't feel right."*
The Inner Wolf's voice was... different. Quieter. Missing its usual manic energy.
*"Something's wrong. My legs feel... weird. Floaty. Like they're not attached properly. Kael, can you feel this? KAEL?"*
Kael reached for the teacup. His hand moved normally. His expression was normal. To anyone watching, the Wolf King was exactly as he always was — cold, composed, untouchable.
But Sera could hear the panic rising in his head like floodwater.

*"I can't feel my fingers. KAEL. KAEL, I CAN'T FEEL MY FINGERS. Try to move them. TRY TO — okay, they moved. But I couldn't FEEL them move. That's bad, right? That's definitely bad. That's POISON bad. That's 'someone put something in my drink' bad and I had wine at dinner last night and — "*
"Alpha."
Sera's voice came out before she could stop it. Kael's golden eyes lifted to hers — cold, questioning, annoyed at the interruption.
*"Why is she talking? She never talks. Well, she talks, but not out of turn. Is something wrong? Is MY FACE wrong? Is my poison face showing?"*
"Your tea," Sera said, her mind racing, "is not to your usual standard. Allow me to bring a fresh pot."
She reached for the cup.
Kael's hand closed around her wrist.

The contact was electric — his skin was hot, always hot, wolf-hot — and Sera's heart lurched into her throat. His grip was gentle but absolute, the kind of hold that said *I could break you and we both know it*.
"The tea is fine," he said.

*"The tea is definitely poisoned. I don't know how I know that but I KNOW that. Let her take it. LET HER TAKE THE POISON TEA, KAEL — wait, no, that's a TERRIBLE idea. She might DRINK it. She might DIE. Why would she die? She's our maid, she wouldn't drink our tea. Would she? No, she's too smart. She's the SMARTEST maid. She's — FOCUS. The poison. The POISON, Fenris."*

Sera's heart was beating so hard she could feel it in her teeth. The Wolf King's hand was still around her wrist. His golden eyes were still fixed on hers. And somewhere in the castle, someone who had poisoned the Alpha was waiting for the effects to kick in.
"Leave it," Kael said, releasing her wrist. "You're dismissed."

*"SHE CAN'T LEAVE. If she leaves and I collapse, who will — no, don't think like that. You're not going to collapse. You're the Alpha. Alphas don't collapse. Alphas don't get poisoned. Except that one time with Uncle Aldric and the wolfsbane and — okay, new plan. New plan: DON'T DIE. That's the plan. Simple. Elegant. Don't die."*

Sera curtsied and retreated toward the door. Every instinct screamed at her to stay — to do *something*, anything — but what could she do? She was a wolf-less maid. She couldn't accuse someone of poisoning the Alpha without evidence. She couldn't fight off assassins. She couldn't —
The door burst open before she reached it.

Three wolves entered. Sera recognized them — pack warriors, mid-ranking, the kind who followed orders without asking questions. Their eyes swept the room, found Kael, and sharpened with an intent that made Sera's blood run cold.
"My Alpha." The leader — a thick-necked wolf named Garrick — bowed with insulting shallowness. "Lord Aldric sends his regards."
Lord Aldric. Kael's exiled uncle. The wolf who had been cast out for challenging Kael's authority three years ago.
*"HOW IS HE HERE?! He was exiled! I watched him CRAWL across the border! Darius! Where's DARIUS?!"*
Kael's expression didn't change. "You have two seconds to explain your presence before I remove your head from your shoulders."

"That's a generous offer." Garrick smiled. "But I'm afraid you won't be removing anything today, Alpha. The wolfsbane extract in your evening wine has already reached your nervous system. Within minutes, you'll be completely paralyzed from the neck down."

*"Wolfsbane. WOLFSBANE. That's the WORST one. That's the one that — okay, okay, I can feel it spreading. My arms are going numb. My — my legs are GONE. I can't feel my legs. KAEL. KAEL, WE'RE IN TROUBLE."*
Sera pressed herself against the wall by the door. She hadn't left. She should have left. Why hadn't she left?

Because Garrick was between her and the exit. Because his companions had fanned out to block the room. Because she was trapped in a study with a paralyzed Alpha and three traitor wolves who had nothing to lose.

"Lord Aldric sends a message," Garrick continued, drawing a blade from his belt. Silver. Moon-blessed — the kind that could kill an Alpha permanently. "The pack is weak under your rule. You shelter wolf-less parasites. You humiliate noble houses. You have forgotten what it means to be Alpha."

*"I'm going to kill him. I'm going to transform and rip his throat out and — I CAN'T TRANSFORM. The wolfsbane is blocking it. I can't — I can't move. ANYTHING. Kael, I can't move ANYTHING. We're going to die. We're going to die and Sera is here and she's going to watch us die and then they're going to KILL HER TOO and — "*
The mental voice cracked. Literally cracked — like ice breaking.

*"Please. Please, someone. Anyone. SAVE HER. I don't care about me. SAVE HER. She didn't do anything wrong. She just brought us TEA. She has MOON-SILVER EYES and she flinches when people are mean to her and she doesn't deserve to DIE in a STUDY with a PARALYZED ALPHA who never even told her — "*
Something inside Sera snapped.

Not her wolf — that was still sealed, still silent, still buried somewhere she couldn't reach. But something else. Something fiercer. The part of her that had survived years of being the pack's punching bag. The part that had learned to be clever because being strong wasn't an option.
*Think.*
She was a wolf-less maid in a room with four wolves. Three enemies, one paralyzed ally. The odds were impossible.
But she had one thing no one else in this room had: she could hear the Alpha's thoughts. She knew exactly what he wanted to do, what he would do if he could move.
And if she couldn't fight for him... she could fight *as* him.
Sera pushed off the wall.
"Gentlemen." Her voice came out steadier than she felt. "I believe you're making a mistake."
Garrick's blade paused mid-arc. His companions turned. All three sets of wolf eyes fixed on the slight, silver-haired maid who had just stepped between them and their prey.
"What did you say, wolf-less?"

Sera smiled. It was not a nice smile. It was the smile of a girl who had spent her entire life being underestimated, and who had just realized that being underestimated was the greatest weapon she possessed.
"I said," she repeated, walking toward Kael's chair with deliberate, unhurried steps, "that you're making a mistake."

*"What is she DOING?! She's going to get herself KILLED. Sera, RUN. Why is she walking TOWARD us? Is she — is she coming to PROTECT us? No one's ever — I mean, Darius would, but Darius isn't HERE and she's a MAID and she's WOLF-LESS and she's — "*
Sera reached Kael's chair.
Then, in full view of three armed traitors, she did the most insane thing she had ever done in her life.
She turned around, lifted the hem of her skirt, and sat directly in the paralyzed Wolf King's lap.

*"SHE'S SITTING ON ME. SHE'S SITTING. ON ME. IN MY LAP. HER — that's her HAIR. Against my CHEST. I can smell lavender. I was RIGHT about the lavender. THIS IS THE BEST DAY OF MY LIFE AND ALSO WE'RE ABOUT TO DIE BUT MOSTLY THE BEST DAY."*

Sera looped one arm around Kael's neck — casual, intimate, like she'd done it a thousand times. She settled her weight against his chest and tilted her head to look at Garrick with an expression of lazy amusement.
"The Alpha," she said, "does not appreciate being interrupted during... private moments."
Garrick's eyes narrowed. "You're his maid."

"I'm whatever he wants me to be." Sera's free hand traced a lazy pattern on Kael's collar. Under her fingertips, she could feel his heartbeat — fast, too fast, wolfsbane-erratic — but his face was still frozen in that mask of cold authority. The paralysis had locked his expression as much as his body.

*"She's — is she pretending to be a — a SEDUCTIVE MAID? She's PRETENDING TO BE MY — I need to play along. I CAN'T play along because I CAN'T MOVE. But I CAN listen. Tell me what to do. Sera. PLEASE. Tell me what to do and I'll do it. Somehow. I'll figure it out. I'll — "*
"You expect me to believe," Garrick said slowly, "that the Wolf King — the coldest Alpha in three generations — is dallying with a *wolf-less servant*?"
"Cold in public." Sera's smile sharpened. "Everyone needs somewhere to be... warm. Isn't that right, my Alpha?"
She turned her head and pressed a kiss to Kael's jaw.
His skin was hot. Burning hot. Wolf-hot and poison-hot and her lips left a tiny damp mark on his stubbled jawline that she would absolutely panic about later.

*"SHE KISSED ME. ON THE JAW. THAT COUNTS. THAT DEFINITELY COUNTS. Fenris, mark the spot. MARK IT. We're never washing that jaw again. Wait, that's unsanitary. We'll wash it but we'll REMEMBER it. We'll remember it FOREVER."*
"Kael," Sera murmured, her lips still close to his ear, her voice pitched to carry, "these men are boring me. Shall I ask them to leave, or would you prefer to do it yourself?"
She waited.
And then, trusting entirely in the voice screaming instructions in her head, she opened her mouth and let Kael's thoughts flow through her.

"Garrick." The word came out in her voice, but the *tone* — the cold, dismissive, utterly Alpha tone — was pure Kael. "You've made three mistakes. The first was believing my uncle's promises. Aldric has never kept a promise in his life — ask the pack elders he betrayed in the border wars."
Garrick's confidence flickered. Just slightly.
"Your second mistake was trusting that wolfsbane would be enough. Do you know how many assassination attempts I've survived? Do you know what happens to wolves who try to poison an Alpha and fail?"
The traitor's blade dipped half an inch.
"Your third mistake..." Sera shifted in Kael's lap, settling more comfortably against his chest, and let her smile turn razor-sharp. "...was assuming I was alone."
The door behind Garrick exploded inward.

Darius Stone filled the doorway, already half-transformed — his grey wolf rippling under his skin, his eyes blazing silver fury. Four more warriors flanked him. The room suddenly felt very, very small.
"Alpha." Darius's voice was a growl. "We found Aldric's camp. The traitors have been dealt with."

*"YES! DARIUS! I KNEW I KEPT HIM AROUND FOR A REASON! Multiple reasons! He's the BEST Beta! Sera, you magnificent, brilliant, terrifying woman — how did you KNOW he was coming?! You knew! YOU KNEW AND YOU STALLED THEM AND YOU SAT IN MY LAP AND YOU — "*
Sera rose from Kael's lap with the grace of someone who did this sort of thing every day. She smoothed her skirt. Adjusted her collar. And looked Garrick directly in the eye.
"The Alpha will see you in the dungeons," she said sweetly. "Assuming Lord Aldric left enough of you to drag there."
Darius's wolves seized the traitors. Garrick's howl of fury was cut short by a fist to the jaw. Within thirty seconds, the study was empty except for Sera, Kael, and Darius.
"The wolfsbane," Darius said, already pulling a vial from his belt. "How bad?"
"Full body paralysis," Sera reported, her voice suddenly drained of the seductive purr she'd been forcing. "He can't move. But his mind is clear. He heard everything."
Darius poured the antidote between Kael's lips. "The healer is on the way. Girl — "
He looked at her. Really looked, for the first time since she'd become the Alpha's maid.
"That was either the bravest or the stupidest thing I've ever seen."
"Both," Sera said faintly. "Definitely both."
And then Kael's fingers twitched — the first sign of returning movement — and something else happened.

Something inside Sera's chest. A heat that wasn't fear or adrenaline. A pulse that didn't belong to her heartbeat. It started at the base of her spine and spread upward, outward, like a second skin trying to burst through her first one.
Her crescent-moon birthmark — the one behind her left ear — burned.
Sera gasped and pressed her hand to the spot. The heat was blinding, consuming, *hungry* — and then it was gone, as quickly as it had come.
But something had changed.
Something deep inside her had *woken up*.

Kael's eyes, still partially paralyzed but burning with golden intensity, found hers across the room. His lips moved. The word was barely a whisper, barely formed, but Sera heard it in stereo — from his mouth and from the frantic, adoring, utterly unhinged voice in his head.
*"Mine."*
And somewhere in the depths of her sealed wolf soul, something answered.
*Soon.*
